verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase subunit beta, F1 complex) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix-facing subunit of the F1Fo-ATP synthase. However, there is well-documented evidence that this protein translocates to the plasma membrane outer leaflet under cancer/stress conditions and during cell-death-related events. Multiple studies have identified ectopic cell-surface ATP synthase beta subunit on tumor cells (acting as an angiostatin receptor and as a target for cancer immunotherapy), qualifying it as a cancer-ecto-protein. This fits cell_state_induced / contextual: intracellular at baseline, surface-accessible in oncogenic/stress states.
